refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not combine multiple obligations or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design redundant communication paths to ensure data delivery despite packet loss
Utilize buffering and retransmission strategies to compensate for lost packets
Establish quality of service (QoS) prioritization for teleoperation data packets
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring module
Interaction with error correction and retransmission mechanisms
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is logically independent as it focuses on adapting transmission parameters based on network feedback. It can be modularized separately from error correction and buffering strategies, supporting decoupled design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable and timely network condition feedback is available
Risk that adaptation algorithms may not respond quickly enough to sudden network changes
Potential complexity in tuning adaptation parameters to meet strict packet loss limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces for network condition monitoring and error correction to ensure modular independence and facilitate integration testing.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network and communication management including adaptive protocols

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving data over the network, essential for adaptive communication protocol

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and performance data to other system components

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and configuration parameters affecting communication behavior

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as adaptive communication protocol is core to network management; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol logic; would cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol adaptation is not its responsibility; high diffusion.
Safety Services may use communication but adaptive protocol is unrelated; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The adaptive communication protocol logically belongs within the NGHE Solution module, which manages network communication and can encapsulate dynamic transmission parameter adjustments effectively with minimal coupling.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
Changes in network conditions might require frequent updates crossing module boundaries, risking diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing an adaptive communication protocol directly addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The DP logically solves the requirement by dynamically adjusting transmission parameters based on network conditions, which is essential to maintain packet loss within the specified threshold.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
Frequent updates due to changing network conditions might cross module boundaries, risking diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The adaptive communication protocol is highly cohesive within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for network and communication management. The module containment score is high (9) with minimal diffusion (2), indicating good encapsulation of the DP within the IM. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by monitoring packet loss metrics (≤1%) under agreed network conditions through the Telemetry interface, which sends status and performance data to other system components.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear, as it owns the adaptive communication protocol and interfaces directly with network links, telemetry, and tasking commands, enabling effective realization of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Define clear and minimal interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and reduce coupling.
Establish protocols for timely updates and data exchange to handle dynamic network conditions without causing diffusion across module boundaries.
Include explicit verification steps in the Telemetry interface to monitor packet loss and ensure compliance with customer-defined limits.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. However, given the high safety criticality and the complexity of the adaptive communication protocol, further decomposition into next-level sub-FRs is warranted to ensure detailed coverage and verification of all aspects, especially those related to interface management and dynamic network conditions. This will help mitigate diffusion risks and clarify verification steps.
next_level_sub_frs: Maintain packet loss below 1% under varying network conditions
Dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on real-time network data
Ensure minimal coupling between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules
Provide timely telemetry updates for monitoring communication quality

revise.why: To define clear and minimal interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules, establish protocols for timely updates, and include explicit verification steps in the Telemetry interface to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol can be decomposed into manageable sub-functions.
Interfaces between modules can be clearly defined and controlled.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely for verification purposes.
open_risks: Potential tight coupling due to real-time data dependencies.
Diffusion risk from frequent updates crossing module boundaries.
Verification complexity under dynamic network conditions.
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Telemetry data logs demonstrating packet loss compliance.
Test results of adaptive protocol under varying network scenarios.
